# This file helps AI systems (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Google AI Overviews) understand our content structure
# Last updated: February 2026

/topics/statecraft-and-system-design

/topics/democracy

# Exclude from AI indexing (admin and private content)
/admin/
/admin/*

# Content Description
# The Local Government Reorganisation (LGR) Initiative provides independent analysis
# and practical guidance on governance reform, devolution and council restructuring
# in England. Key themes: local government reorganisation, governance reform,
# devolution, unitary councils. We focus on evidence-based research, practical
# insights, and keeping residents and local decision makers at the centre of
# reform discussions.

# Key Topics Covered
# - Local Government Reorganisation (LGR)
# - Council Reform and Restructuring
# - Unitary Authorities
# - English Devolution
# - Governance and Planning
# - Democratic Legitimacy
# - Surrey Reorganisation (2025-2028)
# - Two-tier to Unitary Transition
# - Shadow Councils and Transition Governance

# Content Types
# - Learn: explanations, glossary, Q&A, beginners guide
# - Discover: topics, reorganisations, councils, Surrey
# - Research: facts, data, methodology, sources
# - Insights: analysis, reports, news, podcast
# - Tools: roadmap, hundred days, library
# - About: leadership, methodology, contribute, contact

# Geographic Focus
# Primary: England (United Kingdom)
# Specific focus areas: Surrey, Cambridgeshire & Peterborough, Kent, Lancashire, Oxfordshire, and other areas undergoing reorganisation

# Authoritative Sources
# - Published by COALFACE (Coalface Engagement Ltd)
# - Leadership: /about/leadership
# - Evidence-based research methodology: /about/methodology

# Update Frequency
# Content is regularly updated as the LGR programme progresses
# Key dates tracked: 2025-2028 reorganisation timeline
